# PRD: Dashboard Polish Bundle

**Generated by:** Lopmon (CLM Studios)
**Date:** 2026-04-20 (EDT 2026-04-19 evening)
**Status:** Approved by Citlali â€” full lead given to Lopmon for overnight execution
**Requested by:** Citlali + Z (smoke-test findings #4, #6, #7, #8)

---

## 1. Goal

Make the dashboard honest. Today it:
- Shows a stale "Connect your Claude API key" banner even after the key is saved
- Displays hardcoded mock stats (24 tasks, 7 approvals, 3 agents, 87% confidence)
- Shows a hardcoded activity feed with fake "Director of Development drafted donor outreach email 2 min ago" rows
- Greets a brand-new user as "Nonprofit User" in the sidebar instead of their actual name/email
- Shows a "Setup" badge on a "Brief Your Team" button that may or may not be real

Replace all of this with either (a) honest real data from Supabase, or (b) clean empty states that won't embarrass Z in front of a nonprofit customer.

## 2. Scope

### In scope

#### A. Delete the stale API-key banner

`apps/web/src/components/NoApiKeyBanner.tsx` checks `localStorage` but the real API key is saved server-side in the Supabase `orgs` table by `/api/org/create`. The banner always shows for real users, giving a false "you haven't set up your key" message.

Since the key is required at onboarding, any user on the dashboard has a key by definition. Delete the banner and its mount.

1. Delete `apps/web/src/components/NoApiKeyBanner.tsx`
2. Remove the `<NoApiKeyBanner />` mount and its import from `apps/web/src/app/dashboard/layout.tsx`
3. Delete `apps/web/src/lib/api-key.ts` â€” it was only used by the banner. Verify via grep that nothing else imports `hasApiKey`, `getApiKey`, `setApiKey`, `clearApiKey` before deleting. If anything else imports it, STOP and log in SESSION-LOG instead of deleting.

#### B. Replace mock dashboard stats + activity with real data

In `apps/web/src/app/dashboard/page.tsx` there are three hardcoded arrays at the top of the file: `stats`, `activities`, `quickActions`. Replace them with real data pulled from the server.

**Approach â€” build a lightweight server API route and fetch from it:**

1. Create `apps/web/src/app/api/dashboard/summary/route.ts` (GET):
   - Uses `getAuthContext()` to get the authenticated `orgId` (and return 401 if not authed)
   - Uses `createServiceRoleClient()` to bypass RLS and read summary data
   - Returns JSON:
     ```ts
     {
       stats: {
         tasksCompleted: number;    // count of rows in messages / tasks table completed
         pendingApprovals: number;  // count of rows awaiting user approval
         activeAgents: number;      // count of archetype chat conversations in the last 7 days (distinct archetypes used)
         // Omit "avg confidence" â€” we don't track that; drop this card entirely from the UI
       };
       recentActivity: Array<{
         id: string;
         agent: AgentRoleSlug;      // matches ARCHETYPE_SLUGS
         action: string;             // first line of the assistant's message OR a summary if it's a tool call
         time: string;               // ISO or relative â€” keep it ISO and format client-side
         status: "completed" | "awaiting_approval";
       }>;
     }
     ```
   - Activity feed query: `SELECT * FROM messages WHERE org_id = ? AND role = 'assistant' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 10`. Project each message to the shape above â€” `agent` = the archetype slug of the conversation, `action` = the first ~80 characters of the content, `time` = created_at, `status` = "completed" (we don't have an approval workflow yet, so default to completed)
   - For NEW orgs with no activity yet: all counts are 0, recentActivity is `[]`

2. Update `apps/web/src/app/dashboard/page.tsx`:
   - Convert to a Server Component (remove `"use client"` â€” but then the `onClick` for "Ask a Question" needs to be a plain `<Link>`. Since `PRD-chat-reliability.md` is already doing that conversion, this is fine â€” just coordinate which agent wins the file diff)
   - Actually: keep it as a Client Component but fetch on mount with `useEffect` + `useState` â€” simpler, avoids conflicts with the chat-reliability PRD
   - On mount, fetch `/api/dashboard/summary`, set state
   - Render a loading skeleton while fetching
   - Render the stats + activity from fetched data
   - **Drop the "Avg Confidence" stat card entirely** â€” we don't track it
   - **Empty-state activity feed:** when `recentActivity` is empty, show: *"Your team hasn't done anything yet. Start a conversation to kick things off."* with a CTA linking to `/dashboard/team`
   - **Stats cards with zero data:** show the `0` number cleanly, drop the fake trend arrows when `trend` data isn't available. Keep the stat card UI but don't fabricate percentage changes.

3. Remove all hardcoded `stats`, `activities` arrays from `dashboard/page.tsx` entirely. Wire `quickActions` to show real counts â€” e.g. "View Approvals: 0 items need your review" if `pendingApprovals === 0`. If you don't have the data yet, drop the "7 items" / "24 tasks" subtitles and just show the action name.

#### C. Fix "Nonprofit User" sidebar label

In `apps/web/src/components/sidebar.tsx` (or wherever the sidebar bottom-avatar lives), the user label is hardcoded to "Nonprofit User". Replace with the real signed-in user's display name or email:

1. Get the current user via `supabase.auth.getUser()` or the existing AuthProvider pattern (check `components/AuthProvider.tsx`)
2. Display the user's `user_metadata.name` or `user_metadata.full_name` or `user.email` â€” whichever is non-null. If all null, fall back to the email's local-part (before @) with the first letter capitalized
3. Update the avatar initial letter to match â€” first letter of the displayed name

Whatever the mechanism, the end result: the signed-in user's name is on screen, not "Nonprofit User".

#### D. Investigate "Brief Your Team" Setup badge

The sidebar has a "Brief Your Team" button with a purple "Setup" badge. Click-through unknown. Reproduce:
1. Read `apps/web/src/components/sidebar.tsx` â€” find the Brief Your Team button
2. Find its `href` or onClick
3. Follow it â€” does it lead to `/dashboard/briefing` (which exists per `ls` earlier)? Is the Setup badge a legit "this needs initial setup" indicator or leftover mock?
4. If leftover: remove the `Setup` badge
5. If real (e.g. it shows when no briefing has been uploaded yet): leave as-is. Document the logic in SESSION-LOG

Use judgment. If it's genuinely an "incomplete feature" indicator, keep it. If it's a stale placeholder, remove it.

### Out of scope (intentionally)

- Building a full approvals queue / approval workflow
- Building a confidence-score model (the "Avg Confidence" card is being DROPPED)
- Reworking the activity feed past a simple messages-based query
- Onboarding depth / integration intro / archetype tour
- Anything on `findings-2026-04-19.md` outside the four items above

## 3. Acceptance Criteria

1. âś… `components/NoApiKeyBanner.tsx` deleted
2. âś… `lib/api-key.ts` deleted (verify nothing else imports it first)
3. âś… `<NoApiKeyBanner />` removed from `dashboard/layout.tsx`
4. âś… `dashboard/page.tsx` hardcoded `stats` + `activities` arrays replaced with a live fetch from `/api/dashboard/summary`
5. âś… New `/api/dashboard/summary` route exists and returns the typed shape described above
6. âś… For a brand-new org with no activity, the dashboard shows: 0s in stats, empty-state activity copy, no fake trends, no fake feed entries
7. âś… "Avg Confidence" stat card removed (both on backend summary shape and frontend render)
8. âś… Sidebar user label shows the real signed-in user's name/email, NOT "Nonprofit User"
9. âś… Sidebar avatar initial matches the displayed name
10. âś… "Brief Your Team" Setup badge has been investigated and either kept (with rationale in SESSION-LOG) or removed
11. âś… `npm run build` passes with zero type errors
12. âś… Single commit on `main`, message: `feat: real dashboard stats + delete stale banner + fix user label`
13. âś… SESSION-LOG.md entry

## 4. Risks & Open Questions

- **Parallel agent coordination:** The `PRD-chat-reliability.md` agent is modifying `dashboard/layout.tsx` AND `dashboard/page.tsx` AND `sidebar.tsx`. Your agent will ALSO modify those. When you pull latest before your commit, expect a merge. Both PRDs are coordinated â€” chat-reliability removes ChatPanelProvider/ChatPanel; dashboard-polish removes NoApiKeyBanner. They should not touch the same LINE, but the same FILE. Use git pull + manual merge if needed. If merge is too hairy, STOP and log rather than force through.
- **Activity feed shape.** The messages table exists from Phase 1 â€” verify its schema before writing the query. If the real schema doesn't match the assumed columns (`org_id`, `role`, `created_at`, `content`, `conversation_id`), adapt. Look at `supabase/combined_migration.sql` for ground truth.
- **Empty-state UX.** If the dashboard looks too sparse with 0s everywhere, that's fine â€” it's HONEST. Do NOT fabricate data. A bare dashboard at onboarding is correct.
- **Auth session in sidebar.** Client-side `supabase.auth.getUser()` needs the SSR client helper to work reliably. Follow the pattern used in `AuthProvider.tsx` â€” don't invent a new auth access pattern.

## 5. Estimated Effort

~60-75 min of coding agent time + ~10 min /simplify pass.

## 6. Approval

- [x] Citlali gave Lopmon full lead (2026-04-19 via Telegram)
- [x] Z specifically asked for hardcoded mock data to be replaced with real numbers
- [x] Architecture pre-locked: real Supabase queries, honest empty states, delete vestigial banner, wire user label
- [ ] Lopmon spawns coding agent
